commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is explicit maintainer approval of the supported provider and upgrade contracts; there is no discrete automated repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain defect was found: the diff adds no dependency or workflow source changes, keeps Hostinger tokens off argv, validates secure API URLs outside loopback tests, limits response... (sha=0f3e3a7bff6a286e5ac6fc50281954b90ddf9816)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a built-in Hostinger direct Linux SSH-lease provider with purchase gating, API/config/docs/tests, and shared retained-resource lifecycle changes across the CLI and existing providers.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a new provider rather than fixing broken current-main behavior. The relevant evidence is redacted after-fix terminal/API output plus focused provider and lifecycle coverage.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is explicit maintainer approval of the supported provider and upgrade contracts; there is no discrete automated repair to queue.

target.title: feat(provider): add Hostinger VPS leases
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a Hostinger VPS provider for Linux SSH leases, including provider
registration, config/flag wiring, token-safe API client behavior, docs, and
tests.

Safety semantics:
- Hostinger purchases require explicit opt-in with `--hostinger-allow-purchase`
  or `hostinger.allowPurchase=true`.
- API tokens are accepted from environment/config only and are not exposed as
  CLI flags.
- Release is stop-based: Crabbox stops the VPS and removes the local claim, but
  Hostinger billing/subscription ownership remains account-owned.
- Cleanup is conservative: it only acts on canonical Crabbox Hostinger hostnames
  with local claim labels that the shared cleanup gate marks expired or failed.
- No delete/cancel subscription behavior is claimed.

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/213

## Verification

Passed locally:
- `gofmt -w $(git ls-files '*.go')`
- `git diff --check`
- `go test ./internal/providers/hostinger ./internal/providers/all ./internal/cli`
- `go test -race ./...`
- `go vet ./...`
- `go build -trimpath -o bin/crabbox ./cmd/crabbox`
- `bash scripts/check-docs.sh`
- `npm ci --prefix worker`
- `npm run format:check --prefix worker`
- `npm run lint --prefix worker`
- `npm run check --prefix worker`
- `npm test --prefix worker`
- `npm run build --prefix worker`
- `bin/crabbox providers --json`
- `bin/crabbox doctor --provider hostinger`
- `~/.codex/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

Latest follow-up verification after ClawSweeper proof feedback:
- `git diff --check`
- `go test ./internal/providers/hostinger ./internal/providers/all ./internal/cli`
- `go vet ./...`
- `go build -trimpath -o bin/crabbox ./cmd/crabbox`
- `bash scripts/check-docs.sh`
- `HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN=<redacted> bin/crabbox list --provider hostinger --json --refresh --all`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Latest structured review result:
- `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

Review follow-up:
- Addressed https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/pull/216#issuecomment-4636085611 by keeping Hostinger catalog item IDs as string identifiers, serializing setup template/data-center IDs as numeric JSON fields, and adding request-body/non-numeric-ID regression coverage.
- Addressed follow-up review on resolved lease reuse by restoring the stored per-lease SSH key for resolved Hostinger targets.
- Addressed updated review on Hostinger setup defaults by explicitly sending `enable_backups:false` and asserting it in request-body coverage.
- Addressed live proof failure discovered on a vanilla Hostinger Ubuntu VPS by preparing the configured work root and Linux readiness checks before command execution.
- Added `crabbox list --all` CLI forwarding so Hostinger can expose full account inventory, including manually created VPSs, outside the Crabbox-owned default lease view.

## Live Hostinger Proof

Classification: paid existing-VPS run/stop proof passed.

Token handling:
- Token loading used `HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN` from a private local file inside the
  process environment.
- The token was not passed as a CLI flag and is not included in this PR.
- Public output below redacts IPs and account/payment details.

Context:
- A Hostinger KVM 1 VPS was created through hPanel in the same account after the
  earlier API purchase attempt reached Hostinger's payment boundary.
- I did not create a second billable VPS through `warmup` for this update, to
  avoid a duplicate monthly charge.
- The hPanel-created VPS was used to validate final provider behavior for
  resolve, Linux preparation, `run`, API inventory, and `stop`.

Commands run with `bin/crabbox` built from this PR branch:

```sh
bin/crabbox run --provider hostinger --id <hostinger-vm-id> --no-sync -- \
  /bin/sh -lc 'printf "crabbox-hostinger-existing-vm-run-proof\n"; hostname; whoami; uname -s'

bin/crabbox stop --provider hostinger --id <hostinger-vm-id>

bin/crabbox list --provider hostinger --json --refresh --all
```

Redacted run result:

```text
run context:
  provider=hostinger target=linux ssh=root@[ipv4]:22

crabbox-hostinger-existing-vm-run-proof
<hostinger-vm-hostname>
root
Linux

run summary:
  exit=0
```

Redacted stop result:

```text
stopped lease=<hostinger-lease-id> vm=<hostinger-vm-id> name=<hostinger-vm-hostname> billing=still-owned
```

Readback after stop:

```text
Hostinger API:
  id=<hostinger-vm-id>
  hostname=<hostinger-vm-hostname>
  plan=KVM 1
  state=stopped
  actions_lock=unlocked

crabbox list --provider hostinger --json --refresh --all:
  Provider=hostinger
  CloudID=<hostinger-vm-id>
  name=<hostinger-vm-hostname>
  status=stopped
  public_net.ip=[ipv4]
```

Important billing note:
- `crabbox stop` stopped the VPS and removed the local Crabbox claim.
- Hostinger still owns the VPS subscription; this provider intentionally does
  not claim subscription cancellation or deletion.

Earlier mutating API purchase attempt:
- The parent catalog family id `hostingercom-vps-kvm1` failed before creating a
  VM with Hostinger `422 [Billing:2004] Item price not found`; docs/help now
  clarify that the provider requires a priced item id.
- The priced item id `hostingercom-vps-kvm1-usd-1m` reached Hostinger's purchase
  endpoint but returned `402 Payment Required`.
- Post-attempt inventory remained empty, so no disposable VPS existed from that
  API purchase attempt.

## Config And Secret Notes

- Requires `HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN` or `CRABBOX_HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN`.
- The token was loaded locally only and was not committed or included in this PR.
- Required purchase config remains explicit: item id, template id, data center
  id, and purchase opt-in.
target.author: coygeek

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
